message:
fix(frankenterm-core): use raw asupersync Cx in wa-p48pw LabRuntime mux_client tests

The LabRuntime tests added in commit 3efa3a39 (wa-p48pw) were calling
pane_delta_send/mpsc_reserve_send helpers, which internally mint a
Cx via crate::cx::for_testing(). Inside a LabRuntime task that already
has its own Cx, this produces a *different* Cx from the task's Cx and
deadlocks the deterministic scheduler because the reserve awaits on a
permit that will never be signaled.

Fix: add a small lab_send helper that takes the caller's LabRuntime Cx
explicitly and uses the raw asupersync reserve/commit API:

    async fn lab_send(
        cx: &asupersync::Cx,
        tx: &compat_mpsc::Sender<PaneDelta>,
        v: PaneDelta,
    ) {
        tx.reserve(cx).await.expect("reserve lab permit").send(v);
    }

Swap every pane_delta_send(..) call in the labruntime_mux_client
submodule for lab_send(&cx, ..) so the tests exercise the same
two-phase reserve/commit semantics the production helpers wrap, but
using the LabRuntime Cx they actually run under.

Production behavior unchanged — this is test-harness plumbing only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
